-- ssa_contract.lua -- stencil fact-transfer contracts from Stencil IR.
--
-- Contracts are over the same 64-bit ABI as runtime facts, but slot facts are
-- encoded in canonical stencil slot-class space. Generated stencil selector
-- code remaps these masks to actual runtime slots using stencil.slotmaps.

local Sig = require("src.fact_signature")
local Facts = require("src.facts")

local M = {}

local function copy_array(xs)
  local out = {}
  for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
  return out
end

local function slot_number_from_subject(subj)
  local id = subj and subj.id
  if type(id) == "number" then return id end
  if type(id) == "string" then return tonumber(id:match("^R(%d+)$") or id:match("^(%d+)$")) end
  return nil
end

local function remap_fact_to_canonical(f, st)
  if type(f) ~= "table" then return f end
  local slot = slot_number_from_subject(f.subject)
  if slot == nil then return f end
  local cls = st and st.slot_class_by_concrete and st.slot_class_by_concrete[slot]
  if cls == nil then return f end
  local nf = {}
  for k, v in pairs(f) do nf[k] = v end
  nf.subject = Facts.slot("R" .. tostring(cls))
  return nf
end

local function remap_facts(facts, st)
  local out = {}
  for _, f in ipairs(facts or {}) do out[#out + 1] = remap_fact_to_canonical(f, st) end
  return out
end

local function node_kills_all_vm_facts(n)
  if n.effect == "call" or n.op == "ExitBoundary" then return true end
  if n.effect == "heap_write" or n.op == "FieldStore" or n.op == "ArrayStore" then return true end
  if n.effect == "gc_barrier" or n.op == "BarrierCheck" then return true end
  return false
end

local function op_is_hard_exit(n)
  return n.op == "ExitResidual" or n.op == "ExitBoundary" or n.op == "ExitUnlowered"
end

function M.from_result(ssa_result, facts)
  local st = assert(ssa_result and ssa_result.stencil, "ssa_contract requires ssa_result.stencil")
  local selector = Sig.encode(remap_facts(facts or ssa_result.facts or {}, st))
  local checked = Sig.empty()
  local produced = Sig.empty()
  local killed = Sig.empty()
  local deps = {}
  local exits = {}

  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do
    if n.guard and n.guard.fact then
      checked = Sig.bor(checked, Sig.encode({remap_fact_to_canonical(n.guard.fact, st)}))
    end
    for _, d in ipairs(n.deps or {}) do deps[#deps + 1] = d end
    if n.exit then exits[#exits + 1] = {pc = n.source or 0, reason = n.exit.reason or "exit"} end

    if n.op == "StoreSlot" or n.op == "StoreI64Slot" then
      local slot = n.hole and n.hole.role_arg
      killed = Sig.bor(killed, Sig.slot_kill(slot))
      if n.op == "StoreI64Slot" then produced = Sig.bor(produced, Sig.i64_slot(slot)) end
    end

    if node_kills_all_vm_facts(n) then
      killed = Sig.bor(killed, Sig.all_table_and_payload_facts())
    end

    if op_is_hard_exit(n) then
      killed = Sig.bor(killed, Sig.all_slot_facts())
      killed = Sig.bor(killed, Sig.all_table_and_payload_facts())
    end
  end

  local required = Sig.minus(selector, checked)
  return {
    selector_sig = Sig.with_literal(selector),
    required_sig = Sig.with_literal(required),
    checked_sig = Sig.with_literal(checked),
    produced_sig = Sig.with_literal(produced),
    killed_sig = Sig.with_literal(killed),
    deps = copy_array(deps),
    exits = exits,
  }
end

return M

-- stencil_normalize.lua -- canonical form/key/hash for hole-parametric Stencil IR.

local Util = require("src.util")
local Facts = require("src.facts")

local M = {}

local function sorted_keys(t)
  local out = {}
  for k in pairs(t or {}) do out[#out + 1] = k end
  table.sort(out, function(a,b) return tostring(a) < tostring(b) end)
  return out
end

local function args_key(args, n)
  if n and n.hole and not n.hole.semantic then return "" end
  local out = {}
  for _, k in ipairs(sorted_keys(args or {})) do
    local v = args[k]
    if type(v) ~= "table" then out[#out + 1] = tostring(k) .. "=" .. tostring(v) end
  end
  return table.concat(out, ",")
end

local IMPORTANT = {
  LoadSlot = "FRAME_LOAD", StoreSlot = "FRAME_STORE", StoreI64Slot = "STORE_I64_SLOT",
  LoadConst = "LOAD_CONST", ConstI64 = "CONST_I64", ConstI64Hole = "CONST_I64_HOLE", ConstNil = "CONST_NIL", ConstBool = "CONST_BOOL",
  Move = "MOVE", GuardI64 = "I64", GuardTable = "TABLE", GuardShape = "SHAPE", GuardMetatableAbsent = "NO_META",
  GuardCallTarget = "CALL_TARGET", GuardArrayHit = "ARRAY_HIT", GuardBounds = "BOUNDS",
  UnboxI64 = "UNBOX_I64", BoxI64Scratch = "BOX_I64",
  AddI64 = "ADD_I64", SubI64 = "SUB_I64", MulI64 = "MUL_I64", I64BinOp = "I64_BINOP", I64UnaryOp = "I64_UNOP", CmpI64 = "CMP_I64",
  FieldLoad = "FIELD_LOAD", FieldStore = "FIELD_STORE", ArrayLoad = "ARRAY_LOAD", ArrayStore = "ARRAY_STORE", BarrierCheck = "BARRIER",
  ExitResidual = "RESIDUAL", ExitBoundary = "BOUNDARY", ExitUnlowered = "UNLOWERED",
}

local CODEGEN_OP = {
  LoadSlot = "load_slot", StoreSlot = "store_slot", StoreI64Slot = "store_i64_slot",
  LoadConst = "load_const", ConstI64 = "const_i64", ConstI64Hole = "const_i64_hole", ConstNil = "const_nil", ConstBool = "const_bool",
  Move = "move_value", GuardI64 = "guard_i64", GuardTable = "guard_table", GuardShape = "guard_shape", GuardMetatableAbsent = "guard_metatable_absent",
  GuardCallTarget = "guard_call_target", GuardArrayHit = "guard_array_hit", GuardBounds = "guard_bounds",
  UnboxI64 = "unbox_i64", BoxI64Scratch = "box_i64_scratch",
  AddI64 = "add_i64", SubI64 = "sub_i64", MulI64 = "mul_i64", I64BinOp = "i64_binop", I64UnaryOp = "i64_unop", CmpI64 = "cmp_i64",
  FieldLoad = "table_field_load", FieldStore = "table_field_store", ArrayLoad = "table_array_load", ArrayStore = "table_array_store", BarrierCheck = "barrier_check",
  ExitResidual = "residual_boundary", ExitBoundary = "boundary", ExitUnlowered = "unlowered_boundary",
}
M.CODEGEN_OP = CODEGEN_OP

local COMPOSITE_PATTERNS = {
  { name = "FIELD_ADDI_UPDATE", seq = {"LoadSlot","GuardTable","GuardShape","GuardMetatableAbsent","FieldLoad","StoreSlot","GuardI64","UnboxI64","ConstI64Hole","AddI64","StoreI64Slot","LoadSlot","FieldStore"} },
  { name = "FIELD_ADDI_UPDATE", seq = {"LoadSlot","GuardTable","GuardShape","GuardMetatableAbsent","FieldLoad","StoreSlot","GuardI64","UnboxI64","ConstI64","AddI64","StoreI64Slot","LoadSlot","FieldStore"} },
  { name = "FIELD_LOAD_RETURN", seq = {"LoadSlot","GuardTable","GuardShape","GuardMetatableAbsent","FieldLoad","StoreSlot","ExitBoundary"} },
  { name = "SELF_CALL", seq = {"LoadSlot","GuardTable","GuardShape","GuardMetatableAbsent","FieldLoad","StoreSlot","LoadSlot","GuardCallTarget","ExitBoundary"} },
}

local function op_list(st)
  local out = {}
  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do out[#out + 1] = n.op end
  return out
end

local function compress_patterns(ops)
  local out, i = {}, 1
  while i <= #ops do
    local matched = false
    for _, p in ipairs(COMPOSITE_PATTERNS) do
      local ok = true
      if i + #p.seq - 1 > #ops then ok = false end
      if ok then for j, op in ipairs(p.seq) do if ops[i + j - 1] ~= op then ok = false; break end end end
      if ok then out[#out + 1] = p.name; i = i + #p.seq; matched = true; break end
    end
    if not matched then out[#out + 1] = IMPORTANT[ops[i]] or ops[i]; i = i + 1 end
  end
  return out
end

function M.form(st) return compress_patterns(op_list(st)) end

function M.active_codegen_ops(st)
  local out = {}
  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do out[#out + 1] = CODEGEN_OP[n.op] or n.op end
  return out
end

function M.checked_facts(st)
  local seen, out = {}, {}
  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do
    if n.guard and n.guard.fact then
      local f = n.guard.fact
      local k = Facts.guard_key(f)
      if not seen[k] then seen[k] = true; out[#out + 1] = f end
    end
  end
  table.sort(out, function(a,b) return Facts.guard_key(a) < Facts.guard_key(b) end)
  return out
end

function M.checked_fact_names(st)
  local out = {}
  for _, f in ipairs(M.checked_facts(st)) do out[#out + 1] = f.predicate end
  table.sort(out)
  return out
end

function M.deps(st)
  local seen, out = {}, {}
  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do
    for _, d in ipairs(n.deps or {}) do if not seen[d] then seen[d] = true; out[#out + 1] = d end end
    if n.guard and n.guard.fact then
      for _, d in ipairs(n.guard.fact.deps or {}) do if not seen[d] then seen[d] = true; out[#out + 1] = d end end
    end
  end
  table.sort(out)
  return out
end

function M.projection(st)
  local exits, virtuals, reasons = 0, 0, {}
  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do
    if n.exit then
      exits = exits + 1
      reasons[#reasons + 1] = n.exit.reason or n.op or "exit"
      if n.exit.virtual_values then virtuals = virtuals + #n.exit.virtual_values end
    end
  end
  table.sort(reasons)
  return { ok = true, exit_obligations = exits, virtual_values = virtuals, reasons = reasons }
end

local function hole_ref(h)
  if not h then return "" end
  return table.concat({"h", tostring(h.id), h.role_kind or "unknown", tostring(h.role_arg or ""), h.ty or "", h.patchable and "P" or "NP", h.semantic and "S" or "V"}, ":")
end

function M.key(st)
  local vmap, vnext, lines = {}, 0, {}
  local function val(v)
    if not v then return "" end
    if not vmap[v] then vnext = vnext + 1; vmap[v] = "v" .. tostring(vnext) end
    return vmap[v]
  end
  for _, h in ipairs(st.holes or {}) do
    lines[#lines + 1] = table.concat({"HOLE", tostring(h.id), h.role_kind or "unknown", tostring(h.role_arg or ""), h.ty or "", h.patchable and "P" or "NP", h.semantic and "S" or "V"}, ";")
  end
  for _, sm in ipairs(st.slotmaps or {}) do
    lines[#lines + 1] = table.concat({"SLOTMAP", tostring(sm.op_idx), tostring(sm.logical_slot), tostring(sm.field_kind)}, ";")
  end
  for _, n in ipairs(st.ops or {}) do
    local ins, outs = {}, {}
    for _, x in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do ins[#ins + 1] = val(x) end
    for _, x in ipairs(n.outputs or {}) do outs[#outs + 1] = val(x) end
    local guard = n.guard and n.guard.key or ""
    local exit = n.exit and (n.exit.reason or "exit") or ""
    lines[#lines + 1] = table.concat({"OP", n.op, table.concat(ins, ","), table.concat(outs, ","), n.effect or "none", hole_ref(n.hole), guard, exit, args_key(n.args, n)}, ";")
  end
  return table.concat(lines, "|")
end

function M.hash(st)
  local facts = {}
  for _, f in ipairs(M.checked_facts(st)) do facts[#facts + 1] = Facts.guard_key(f) end
  return Util.stable_hash(M.key(st) .. " :: " .. table.concat(facts, ",") .. " :: " .. table.concat(M.deps(st), ","))
end

return M

-- materialize_native_x64.lua -- mechanical copy/link/patch for SpongeJIT x86-64 native stencils.
--
-- This module consumes already-built native stencil descriptors. It never runs
-- SSA, never normalizes, and never infers hidden semantics. Per-site variation
-- enters only through explicit relocation records plus caller-provided concrete
-- bytecode/slot/constant bindings.

local M = {}

local RELOC_KIND_BY_ID = {
  [0] = "unknown", [1] = "abs32", [2] = "abs32s", [3] = "plt32", [4] = "pc32", [5] = "abs64", [6] = "abs8",
}

local DATA_ROLE_BY_ID = {
  [1] = "slot", [2] = "slot_store", [3] = "imm", [4] = "const", [5] = "bool",
  [6] = "shape_offset", [7] = "shape_id", [8] = "metatable_offset", [9] = "field_offset",
  [10] = "array_base_offset", [11] = "call_target", [12] = "barrier",
}

local CONTROL_EDGE_BY_ID = {
  [1] = "fallthrough", [2] = "guard_fail", [3] = "residual", [4] = "boundary", [5] = "projection_stub",
}

local function kind_name(v, by_id)
  if type(v) == "string" then return v end
  return by_id[tonumber(v) or -1] or tostring(v)
end

local function copy_array(xs)
  local out = {}
  for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
  return out
end

local function hex_to_array(hex)
  assert(type(hex) == "string" and #hex % 2 == 0, "native stencil code_hex must be an even-length hex string")
  local out = {}
  for pair in hex:gmatch("%x%x") do out[#out + 1] = tonumber(pair, 16) or 0 end
  return out
end

local function array_to_string(bytes)
  local out = {}
  for i, b in ipairs(bytes or {}) do out[i] = string.char(b % 256) end
  return table.concat(out)
end

local function string_to_array(s)
  local out = {}
  for i = 1, #s do out[i] = s:byte(i) end
  return out
end

local function patch_u8(bytes, off, v)
  bytes[off + 1] = (tonumber(v) or 0) % 256
end

local function patch_u32(bytes, off, v)
  local n = tonumber(v) or 0
  if n < 0 then n = n + 4294967296 end
  bytes[off + 1] = n % 256
  bytes[off + 2] = math.floor(n / 256) % 256
  bytes[off + 3] = math.floor(n / 65536) % 256
  bytes[off + 4] = math.floor(n / 16777216) % 256
end

local function patch_i64(bytes, off, v)
  local n = tonumber(v) or 0
  local neg = n < 0
  if neg then n = -n end
  local b = {}
  for i = 1, 8 do b[i] = n % 256; n = math.floor(n / 256) end
  if neg then
    local carry = 1
    for i = 1, 8 do
      local x = 255 - b[i] + carry
      if x >= 256 then x = x - 256; carry = 1 else carry = 0 end
      b[i] = x
    end
  end
  for i = 1, 8 do bytes[off + i] = b[i] end
end

local function op_at(entry, op_idx)
  return (entry.ops or entry.source_ops or {})[(tonumber(op_idx) or 0) + 1] or {}
end

local function op_name(op)
  if type(op) == "table" then return tostring(op.op or "") end
  return tostring(op or "")
end

local function logical_slot(entry, role_arg)
  local logical = tonumber(role_arg) or 0
  local slots = entry.actual_slots or entry.slot_bindings or entry.slots or {}
  if slots[logical] ~= nil then return tonumber(slots[logical]) or logical end
  if slots[tostring(logical)] ~= nil then return tonumber(slots[tostring(logical)]) or logical end
  return logical
end

local function default_imm_from_op(op)
  if type(op) ~= "table" then return 0 end
  if op.c ~= nil then return tonumber(op.c) or 0 end
  if op.sC ~= nil then return tonumber(op.sC) or 0 end
  if op.b ~= nil then return tonumber(op.b) or 0 end
  if op.sB ~= nil then return tonumber(op.sB) or 0 end
  if op.sBx ~= nil then return tonumber(op.sBx) or 0 end
  if op.sJ ~= nil then return tonumber(op.sJ) or 0 end
  return 0
end

local function imm_value(entry, r)
  local op_idx = tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0
  local imms = entry.immediates or {}
  if imms[op_idx] ~= nil then return imms[op_idx] end
  if imms[tostring(op_idx)] ~= nil then return imms[tostring(op_idx)] end
  return default_imm_from_op(op_at(entry, op_idx))
end

local function const_index(entry, r)
  local op_idx = tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0
  local consts = entry.const_slots or entry.constants or {}
  if consts[op_idx] ~= nil then return tonumber(consts[op_idx]) or 0 end
  if consts[tostring(op_idx)] ~= nil then return tonumber(consts[tostring(op_idx)]) or 0 end
  local op = op_at(entry, op_idx)
  if type(op) == "table" then return tonumber(op.bx or op.ax or op.b or op.c or op.const or 0) or 0 end
  return 0
end

local function bool_tag(entry, r)
  local op_idx = tonumber(r.op_idx) or 0
  local bools = entry.booleans or {}
  local v = bools[op_idx]
  if v == nil then v = bools[tostring(op_idx)] end
  if v == nil then
    local name = op_name(op_at(entry, op_idx))
    if name == "LOADFALSE" or name == "LFALSESKIP" then v = false else v = true end
  end
  return v and 17 or 1 -- PUC Lua 5.5 LUA_VTRUE / LUA_VFALSE tags.
end

local function data_patch_value(entry, r)
  local role = kind_name(r.role_kind, DATA_ROLE_BY_ID)
  local addend = tonumber(r.addend) or 0
  if role == "slot" or role == "slot_store" then
    return logical_slot(entry, r.role_arg) * 16 + addend
  elseif role == "imm" then
    return imm_value(entry, r) + addend
  elseif role == "const" then
    return const_index(entry, r) * 16 + addend
  elseif role == "bool" then
    return bool_tag(entry, r) + addend
  end
  error("unsupported materializer data role: " .. tostring(role))
end

local function patch_data_reloc(bytes, base, entry, r)
  local reloc = kind_name(r.reloc_kind, RELOC_KIND_BY_ID)
  local off = base + (tonumber(r.code_offset) or 0)
  local v = data_patch_value(entry, r)
  if reloc == "abs8" then patch_u8(bytes, off, v)
  elseif reloc == "abs32" or reloc == "abs32s" then patch_u32(bytes, off, v)
  elseif reloc == "abs64" then patch_i64(bytes, off, v)
  elseif reloc == "unknown" then error("native data reloc has unknown encoding at offset " .. tostring(off))
  else error("unsupported materializer data reloc encoding: " .. tostring(reloc)) end
end

local function normalize_entry(e)
  if e.stencil then return e end
  return { stencil = e }
end

function M.materialize(entries, opts)
  opts = opts or {}
  assert(type(entries) == "table" and #entries > 0, "materialize requires at least one stencil entry")
  local norm = {}
  local image = {}
  local offset = 0

  for i, raw in ipairs(entries) do
    local entry = normalize_entry(raw)
    local st = assert(entry.stencil, "entry missing stencil")
    if st.executable ~= true then error("cannot materialize non-executable stencil at entry " .. tostring(i)) end
    if type(st.code_hex) ~= "string" or #st.code_hex == 0 then error("native stencil missing code_hex at entry " .. tostring(i)) end
    local bytes = hex_to_array(st.code_hex)
    norm[i] = {
      stencil = st,
      ops = entry.ops or st.source_ops or {},
      source_ops = entry.source_ops or st.source_ops or {},
      actual_slots = entry.actual_slots or entry.slot_bindings or entry.slots,
      slot_bindings = entry.slot_bindings,
      immediates = entry.immediates,
      const_slots = entry.const_slots,
      constants = entry.constants,
      booleans = entry.booleans,
      base = offset,
      size = #bytes,
    }
    for _, b in ipairs(bytes) do image[#image + 1] = b end
    offset = offset + #bytes
  end

  for _, entry in ipairs(norm) do
    for _, r in ipairs(entry.stencil.data_relocs or {}) do patch_data_reloc(image, entry.base, entry, r) end
  end

  for i, entry in ipairs(norm) do
    for _, r in ipairs(entry.stencil.control_relocs or {}) do
      local edge = kind_name(r.edge_kind, CONTROL_EDGE_BY_ID)
      local reloc = kind_name(r.reloc_kind, RELOC_KIND_BY_ID)
      if edge == "fallthrough" and i < #norm then
        if reloc ~= "pc32" then error("fallthrough control reloc must be pc32") end
        local site = entry.base + (tonumber(r.code_offset) or 0)
        local target = norm[i + 1].base
        patch_u32(image, site, target - (site + 4))
      end
    end
  end

  local bytes = array_to_string(image)
  return {
    ok = true,
    bytes = bytes,
    code_size = #bytes,
    entries = norm,
  }
end

function M.alloc_executable(bytes)
  local ok, ffi = pcall(require, "ffi")
  if not ok then return nil, "ffi unavailable" end
  ffi.cdef[[
    typedef unsigned long size_t;
    void *mmap(void *addr, size_t length, int prot, int flags, int fd, long offset);
    int munmap(void *addr, size_t length);
    void *memcpy(void *dest, const void *src, size_t n);
  ]]
  local PROT_READ, PROT_WRITE, PROT_EXEC = 1, 2, 4
  local MAP_PRIVATE, MAP_ANON = 2, 0x20
  local n = #bytes
  local mem = ffi.C.mmap(nil, n, PROT_READ + PROT_WRITE + PROT_EXEC, MAP_PRIVATE + MAP_ANON, -1, 0)
  if mem == ffi.cast("void *", -1) then return nil, "mmap failed" end
  local src = ffi.new("uint8_t[?]", n)
  local arr = string_to_array(bytes)
  for i, b in ipairs(arr) do src[i - 1] = b end
  ffi.C.memcpy(mem, src, n)
  return mem, n
end

function M.free_executable(mem, n)
  local ok, ffi = pcall(require, "ffi")
  if not ok then return nil, "ffi unavailable" end
  return ffi.C.munmap(mem, n) == 0
end

M._patch_u32 = patch_u32
M._patch_i64 = patch_i64
M._hex_to_array = hex_to_array
M._array_to_string = array_to_string

return M

-- ssa_atoms.lua -- atom semantic reopening from serialized/codegen op lists.

local Facts = require("src.facts")
local IR = require("src.ssa_ir")

local M = {}

local function copy_array(xs)
    local out = {}
    for i, x in ipairs(xs or {}) do out[i] = x end
    return out
end

local function copy_map(t)
    local out = {}
    for k, v in pairs(t or {}) do out[k] = v end
    return out
end

function M.reopen_node_specs(specs, facts, config)
    local fs = getmetatable(facts) == Facts.FactSet and facts or Facts.new(facts or {})
    local g = IR.new(fs, config)
    local vmap = {}

    local function mapped_input(old, ty, residency)
        if not old then return nil end
        if residency == false then residency = nil end
        if not vmap[old] then vmap[old] = g:new_value(ty or "Unknown", nil, nil, residency) end
        return vmap[old]
    end

    local function mapped_outputs(spec)
        local outs = {}
        for i, old in ipairs(spec.outputs or {}) do
            local ty = (spec.output_types and spec.output_types[i]) or "Unknown"
            local residency = spec.output_residencies and spec.output_residencies[i]
            if residency == false then residency = nil end
            local nv = g:new_value(ty, nil, nil, residency)
            vmap[old] = nv
            outs[#outs + 1] = nv
        end
        if outs[#outs] then g.current = outs[#outs] end
        return outs
    end

    for pc, spec in ipairs(specs or {}) do
        local ins = {}
        for i, old in ipairs(spec.inputs or {}) do
            local ty = spec.input_types and spec.input_types[i]
            local residency = spec.input_residencies and spec.input_residencies[i]
            ins[#ins + 1] = mapped_input(old, ty, residency)
        end
        local outs = mapped_outputs(spec)
        local exit = spec.exit and copy_map(spec.exit) or nil
        if not exit then
            if spec.effect == "guard" then exit = g:exit_projection("guard:" .. tostring(spec.guard_fact and spec.guard_fact.predicate or spec.op), spec.source or pc)
            elseif spec.op == "Residual" or spec.op == "Call" or spec.op == "KnownCall" or spec.op == "TailCall" then exit = g:exit_projection(tostring(spec.op) .. "_exit", spec.source or pc) end
        end
        g:add(spec.op or spec.codegen_op, {
            inputs = ins,
            outputs = outs,
            args = spec.args or {},
            effect = spec.effect or "none",
            codegen_op = spec.codegen_op,
            guard = spec.guard_fact and { fact = spec.guard_fact, key = Facts.guard_key(spec.guard_fact) } or nil,
            deps = copy_array(spec.deps),
            exit = exit,
            mem_in = copy_map(spec.mem_in),
            mem_out = copy_map(spec.mem_out),
            source = spec.source or pc,
        })
    end
    return g
end

return M

-- ssa_validate.lua -- invariant checks for real fact/SSA layer.

local M = {}

local function add(errors, msg) errors[#errors + 1] = msg end

function M.validate(g, config)
    config = config or {}
    local errors = {}
    if g.invalid then
        for _, r in ipairs(g.invalid_reasons or {}) do add(errors, r) end
        return false, errors
    end
    local ok_graph, graph_errors = g:validate()
    if not ok_graph then for _, e in ipairs(graph_errors) do add(errors, e) end end

    for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
        if not n.removed then
            if n.effect == "guard" then
                if not n.guard or not n.guard.fact then add(errors, "guard node missing fact at " .. n.id) end
                if not n.exit then add(errors, "guard node missing exit projection at " .. n.id) end
            end
            if (n.op == "Residual" or n.op == "GenericExit" or n.op == "Call" or n.op == "KnownCall" or n.op == "TailCall") and not n.exit then
                add(errors, n.op .. " missing exit projection at " .. n.id)
            end
            if n.op == "FrameLoad" and not (n.args and n.args.slot) then add(errors, "FrameLoad missing slot at " .. n.id) end
            if n.op == "FrameStore" and not (n.args and n.args.slot) then add(errors, "FrameStore missing slot at " .. n.id) end
        end
    end

    return #errors == 0, errors
end

function M.lowerable(g, cover_set)
    local errors = {}
    for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
        if not n.removed then
            local op = n.codegen_op or n.op
            if cover_set and not cover_set[op] then add(errors, "no stencil cover for " .. tostring(op) .. " at node " .. n.id) end
        end
    end
    return #errors == 0, errors
end

return M

-- ssa_opt.lua -- typed SSA optimization over explicit values and memory.

local IR = require("src.ssa_ir")

local M = {}

local function starts(s, prefix) return tostring(s or ""):sub(1, #prefix) == prefix end

local function outputs_of(n) return n.outputs or {} end
local function inputs_of(n) return n.inputs or {} end

local function replace_inputs(g, alias)
    local function root(v)
        local seen = {}
        while alias[v] and not seen[v] do seen[v] = true; v = alias[v] end
        return v
    end
    for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
        for i, v in ipairs(n.inputs or {}) do n.inputs[i] = root(v) end
    end
end

local function result_producer(g)
    local prod = {}
    for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
        for _, v in ipairs(outputs_of(n)) do prod[v] = n end
    end
    return prod
end

local function use_counts(g)
    local used = {}
    for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
        if not n.removed then
            for _, v in ipairs(inputs_of(n)) do used[v] = (used[v] or 0) + 1 end
        end
    end
    return used
end

local function remove(g, n, why)
    if not n.removed then
        n.removed = true
        n.remove_reason = why
        g.stats.removed = (g.stats.removed or 0) + 1
    end
end

local function pass_copy_forward(g)
    local alias = {}
    for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
        if not n.removed and n.op == "Move" and n.inputs[1] and n.outputs[1] then
            alias[n.outputs[1]] = n.inputs[1]
            remove(g, n, "copy_forward")
        end
    end
    replace_inputs(g, alias)
end

local function pass_box_unbox(g)
    local prod = result_producer(g)
    local alias = {}
    for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
        if not n.removed and n.op == "UnboxI64" and n.inputs[1] and n.outputs[1] then
            local p = prod[n.inputs[1]]
            if p and not p.removed and p.op == "BoxI64" and p.inputs[1] then
                -- Check if this BoxI64 output is also consumed by a FrameStore
                -- (meaning it will be fused at codegen). If so, don't eliminate
                -- the UnboxI64 — the codegen handles forwarding via the
                -- forwarded[] table, and removing the UnboxI64 can confuse
                -- downstream C compilers (register reuse of stale values).
                local has_frame_store = false
                for _, m in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
                    if not m.removed and m.op == "FrameStore" then
                        for _, vid in ipairs(m.inputs or {}) do
                            if vid == n.inputs[1] then has_frame_store = true; break end
                        end
                    end
                    if has_frame_store then break end
                end
                if not has_frame_store then
                    alias[n.outputs[1]] = p.inputs[1]
                    remove(g, n, "box_unbox")
                end
            end
        elseif not n.removed and n.op == "BoxI64" and n.inputs[1] and n.outputs[1] then
            local p = prod[n.inputs[1]]
            if p and not p.removed and p.op == "UnboxI64" and p.inputs[1] then
                alias[n.outputs[1]] = p.inputs[1]
                remove(g, n, "unbox_box")
            end
        end
    end
    replace_inputs(g, alias)
end

local function pass_guard_dominance(g)
    local seen = {}
    for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
        if not n.removed and n.effect == "guard" and n.guard then
            local k = n.guard.key or (n.op .. ":" .. tostring(n.inputs[1]))
            if seen[k] then remove(g, n, "dominated_guard") else seen[k] = true end
        end
        if not n.removed and IR.HARD_BARRIER[n.op] then
            -- Calls/residuals can invalidate facts not covered by dependency epochs.
            -- Keep shape/metatable/call target guards dominated across code only if deps remain valid;
            -- for now, be conservative and clear domination at hard VM boundaries.
            seen = {}
        end
    end
end

local function pass_frame_forward(g)
    -- Real frame forwarding: slot identity, not adjacency. A FrameStore(slot, v)
    -- makes later FrameLoad(slot) alias v until another store/barrier invalidates it.
    local last_store = {}
    local alias = {}
    for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
        if n.removed then goto continue end
        if n.op == "FrameStore" then
            local slot = tostring((n.args and n.args.slot) or "cur")
            last_store[slot] = n.inputs[1]
        elseif n.op == "FrameLoad" then
            local slot = tostring((n.args and n.args.slot) or "cur")
            local v = last_store[slot]
            if v and n.outputs[1] then
                while alias[v] do v = alias[v] end
                alias[n.outputs[1]] = v
                remove(g, n, "frame_load_forward")
            end
        elseif IR.HARD_BARRIER[n.op] then
            last_store = {}
        end
        ::continue::
    end
    replace_inputs(g, alias)
end

local function pass_dead_frame_store(g)
    -- A frame store is dead if the same slot is overwritten before any load or
    -- hard boundary. This is correct because exit projection at a hard boundary
    -- may need the frame, so boundaries stop the analysis.
    for i, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
        if not n.removed and n.op == "FrameStore" then
            local slot = tostring((n.args and n.args.slot) or "cur")
            local used = false
            for j = i + 1, #g.nodes do
                local m = g.nodes[j]
                if m.removed then goto continue_dead end
                if IR.HARD_BARRIER[m.op] or m.effect == "return_" or m.effect == "guard" or m.effect == "branch" then used = true; break end
                if m.op == "FrameLoad" and tostring((m.args and m.args.slot) or "cur") == slot then used = true; break end
                if m.op == "FrameStore" and tostring((m.args and m.args.slot) or "cur") == slot then break end
                ::continue_dead::
            end
            if not used then remove(g, n, "dead_frame_store") end
        end
    end
end

local function same_field(a, b)
    return a and b and a.args and b.args and tostring(a.args.key) == tostring(b.args.key)
end

local function pass_field_forward(g)
    -- If FieldStore(table,key,v) is followed by FieldLoad(same table,key) before
    -- another heap write/call/residual, forward v. Shape/key facts make this exact.
    local alias = {}
    local last = nil
    for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
        if n.removed then goto continue end
        if n.op == "FieldStore" then
            last = n
        elseif n.op == "FieldLoad" and last and same_field(last, n) and last.inputs[1] == n.inputs[1] then
            if n.outputs[1] and last.inputs[2] then
                alias[n.outputs[1]] = last.inputs[2]
                remove(g, n, "field_load_forward")
            end
        elseif n.effect == "heap_write" or IR.HARD_BARRIER[n.op] then
            last = nil
        end
        ::continue::
    end
    replace_inputs(g, alias)
end

local function pass_barrier_elim(g)
    if not g.factset or not g.factset:implies("barrier_clean") then return end
    for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
        if not n.removed and n.op == "BarrierCheck" then remove(g, n, "barrier_clean") end
    end
end

local function pass_constant_fold(g)
    local prod = result_producer(g)
    local alias = {}
    for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
        if not n.removed and (n.op == "AddI64" or n.op == "SubI64" or n.op == "MulI64") then
            local a, b = prod[n.inputs[1]], prod[n.inputs[2]]
            if a and b and a.op == "ConstI64" and b.op == "ConstI64" and a.args and b.args then
                local av, bv = tonumber(a.args.value or 0) or 0, tonumber(b.args.value or 0) or 0
                local r = n.op == "AddI64" and (av + bv) or (n.op == "SubI64" and (av - bv) or (av * bv))
                n.op = "ConstI64"
                n.codegen_op = "const_i64"
                n.inputs = {}
                n.args = { value = r }
                n.effect = "none"
            end
        end
    end
    replace_inputs(g, alias)
end

local function pass_dce(g)
    local changed = true
    while changed do
        changed = false
        local used = use_counts(g)
        for _, n in ipairs(g.nodes or {}) do
            if not n.removed and IR.PURE_OP[n.op] then
                local all_dead = true
                for _, v in ipairs(outputs_of(n)) do if (used[v] or 0) > 0 then all_dead = false; break end end
                if all_dead and #(outputs_of(n)) > 0 then remove(g, n, "dead_value"); changed = true end
            end
        end
    end
end

function M.optimize(g, config)
    config = config or {}
    if g.invalid then return g end
    -- Only pass_frame_forward (GCC can't alias HOLE references) and
    -- pass_guard_dominance (GCC doesn't know Lua guard semantics).
    -- Everything else (copy prop, box/unbox, dead store, constant fold, DCE)
    -- GCC does better than our SSA optimizer.
    pass_frame_forward(g)
    pass_guard_dominance(g)
    pass_barrier_elim(g)
    pass_field_forward(g)
    return g
end

return M

-- worker_compile.lua — One parallel worker: SSA compile + stencil normal forms.
-- Usage: luajit src/worker_compile.lua <chunk_id>
--
-- Workers run the foundry semantic pass only:
--   opcode sequence + facts -> SSA -> canonical normal form.
-- They do not lower descriptors. Global unique final forms are merged and lowered
-- once by dedupe_normal_forms.lua before C bank generation.

package.path = 'src/?.lua;src/?/init.lua;' .. package.path

local cjson = require("cjson")
local sqlite3 = require("lsqlite3")
local SSA = require("src.ssa")
local Contract = require("src.ssa_contract")
local Util = require("src.util")
local FactAxes = require("src.ssa_fact_axes")

local ci = tonumber(arg[1])
assert(ci, "usage: luajit src/worker_compile.lua <chunk_id>")

local config = {
  max_fact_combos = tonumber(os.getenv("MAX_FACT_COMBOS") or "0") or 0,
  fact_axis_mode = tostring(os.getenv("FACT_AXIS_MODE") or "curated"),
}
local progress_every = tonumber(os.getenv("WORKER_PROGRESS_SEQS") or "1000") or 1000

local tmpdir = os.getenv("SPON_TMP") or "/tmp"
local function tmp(name) return tmpdir .. "/" .. name end
local seqs = Util.read_json(tmp("grammar_chunk_" .. ci .. ".json"))

io.stderr:write(string.format("[W%d] START seqs=%d fact_mode=%s max_fact_combos=%s\n",
  ci, #seqs, config.fact_axis_mode, tostring(config.max_fact_combos)))

local function op_signature(ops)
  local parts = {}
  for _, op in ipairs(ops or {}) do parts[#parts + 1] = type(op) == "table" and tostring(op.op) or tostring(op) end
  return table.concat(parts, "|")
end

local function contract_key(contract)
  return table.concat({
    tostring(contract.selector_sig and contract.selector_sig.literal or ""),
    tostring(contract.required_sig and contract.required_sig.literal or ""),
    tostring(contract.checked_sig and contract.checked_sig.literal or ""),
    tostring(contract.produced_sig and contract.produced_sig.literal or ""),
    tostring(contract.killed_sig and contract.killed_sig.literal or ""),
  }, "|")
end

local db_path = tmp("grammar_worker_" .. ci .. ".sqlite")
os.remove(db_path)
local db = assert(sqlite3.open(db_path))
assert(db:exec([[
PRAGMA journal_mode = OFF;
PRAGMA synchronous = OFF;
PRAGMA temp_store = MEMORY;
PRAGMA locking_mode = EXCLUSIVE;
CREATE TABLE forms (
  dedupe_key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  stencil_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
  contract_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  selector_sig TEXT NOT NULL,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  form_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  ssa_json TEXT NOT NULL
);
CREATE TABLE patterns (
  dedupe_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  ops_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  ops_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY(dedupe_key, ops_key)
);
CREATE TABLE stats (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  value INTEGER NOT NULL
);
BEGIN IMMEDIATE;
]]) == sqlite3.OK)

local insert_form = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO forms
  (dedupe_key, stencil_hash, contract_key, selector_sig, count, form_json, ssa_json)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)]]))
local bump_form = assert(db:prepare([[UPDATE forms SET count = count + 1 WHERE dedupe_key = ?]]))
local insert_pattern = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO patterns
  (dedupe_key, ops_key, ops_json, count) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
  ON CONFLICT(dedupe_key, ops_key) DO UPDATE SET count = count + excluded.count]]))

local function step_done(stmt)
  local rc = stmt:step()
  if rc ~= sqlite3.DONE then error(db:errmsg()) end
  stmt:reset()
end

local forms_seen = {}
local lc, lssa_ok, nforms = 0, 0, 0

for si, ops in ipairs(seqs) do
  local subsets = FactAxes.subsets(FactAxes.axes_for_ops(ops), config)
  for _, facts in ipairs(subsets) do
    local r = SSA.compile(ops, facts, config)
    lc = lc + 1
    if r.ok then
      lssa_ok = lssa_ok + 1
      local contract = Contract.from_result(r, facts)
      local ckey = contract_key(contract)
      local normal_key = tostring(r.stencil_hash)
      -- Equivalence is the SSA-consumed canonical normal form. The contract is
      -- retained as descriptor metadata for the chosen representative, but it is
      -- not part of the dedupe key: source facts/opcodes are aliases of the form.
      local dedupe_key = normal_key
      local ops_key = op_signature(ops)

      insert_pattern:bind_values(dedupe_key, ops_key, cjson.encode(ops), 1)
      step_done(insert_pattern)

      if forms_seen[dedupe_key] then
        bump_form:bind_values(dedupe_key)
        step_done(bump_form)
      else
        nforms = nforms + 1
        forms_seen[dedupe_key] = true
        local form = {
          key = normal_key,
          dedupe_key = dedupe_key,
          stencil_hash = r.stencil_hash,
          stencil_form = r.stencil_form,
          stencil_key = r.stencil_key,
          stencil_ops = r.stencil_ops,
          stencil_slotmaps = r.slotmaps,
          ops = ops,
          facts = facts,
          contract = contract,
          count = 1,
          changed = r.changed,
          source_ops = ops,
        }
        local ssa_payload = {
          stencil = r.stencil,
          stencil_hash = r.stencil_hash,
          stencil_key = r.stencil_key,
          stencil_ops = r.stencil_ops,
          slotmaps = r.slotmaps,
          active_node_specs = r.active_node_specs,
          checked_facts = r.checked_facts,
          deps = r.deps,
          projection = r.projection,
          source_ops = ops,
          facts = facts,
        }
        local selector_sig = tostring(contract.selector_sig and contract.selector_sig.literal or "0x0000000000000000ULL")
        insert_form:bind_values(dedupe_key, normal_key, ckey, selector_sig, 1, cjson.encode(form), cjson.encode(ssa_payload))
        step_done(insert_form)
      end
    end
  end
  if progress_every > 0 and (si % progress_every == 0 or si == #seqs) then
    io.stderr:write(string.format("[W%d] %d/%d compiles=%d ssa_ok=%d unique_forms=%d\n", ci, si, #seqs, lc, lssa_ok, nforms))
  end
end

insert_form:finalize()
bump_form:finalize()
insert_pattern:finalize()
local stats = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO stats(key, value) VALUES (?, ?)]]))
local ssa_rejected = lc - lssa_ok
for _, kv in ipairs({
  {"seqs", #seqs},
  {"compiles", lc},
  {"ok", lssa_ok},
  {"ssa_ok", lssa_ok},
  {"rejected", ssa_rejected},
  {"forms", nforms},
  {"stencils", 0},
}) do
  stats:bind_values(kv[1], kv[2])
  step_done(stats)
end
stats:finalize()
assert(db:exec("COMMIT; CREATE INDEX forms_stencil_hash_idx ON forms(stencil_hash); CREATE INDEX patterns_ops_key_idx ON patterns(ops_key);") == sqlite3.OK)
db:close()

io.stderr:write(string.format("[W%d] DONE seqs=%d compiles=%d ssa_ok=%d rejected=%d unique_forms=%d db=%s\n", ci, #seqs, lc, lssa_ok, ssa_rejected, nforms, db_path))

-- dedupe_normal_forms.lua -- Global foundry normal-form merge + unique lowering.
--
-- This is the semantic boundary that the monolithic foundry implies:
--   opcode sequence + facts -> SSA consumes semantics -> canonical normal form
--   -> global equivalence class -> lower one representative to the descriptor bank.
--
-- Per-worker DBs contain normal forms and pattern aliases. This script merges
-- equivalent forms across workers, lowers each unique final form once, and keeps
-- all source-pattern aliases so the C selector can map many opcode sequences to
-- the same unique bank id.

package.path = 'src/?.lua;src/?/init.lua;' .. package.path

local cjson = require("cjson")
local sqlite3 = require("lsqlite3")
local Util = require("src.util")
local StencilLower = require("src.stencil_lower")
local StencilDesc = require("src.stencil_desc")

local tmpdir = arg[1] or os.getenv("SPON_TMP") or "build/stencil_bank"
local chunks = tonumber(arg[2] or os.getenv("CHUNKS") or "128") or 128
local out_path = arg[3] or (tmpdir .. "/stencil_bank.sqlite")

local function worker_path(ci) return tmpdir .. "/grammar_worker_" .. ci .. ".sqlite" end

local function step_done_for(db, stmt)
  local rc = stmt:step()
  if rc ~= sqlite3.DONE then error(db:errmsg()) end
  stmt:reset()
end

local function numeric_key_map(t)
  if type(t) ~= "table" then return t end
  local out = {}
  for k, v in pairs(t) do out[tonumber(k) or k] = v end
  return out
end

local function rehydrate_ssa_payload(p)
  p = p or {}
  local st = p.stencil or {}
  st.slot_class_by_concrete = numeric_key_map(st.slot_class_by_concrete)
  st.slot_concrete_by_class = st.slot_concrete_by_class or {}
  p.stencil = st
  return p
end

os.remove(out_path)
local db = assert(sqlite3.open(out_path))
assert(db:exec([[
PRAGMA journal_mode = OFF;
PRAGMA synchronous = OFF;
PRAGMA temp_store = MEMORY;
PRAGMA locking_mode = EXCLUSIVE;
CREATE TABLE unique_forms (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  dedupe_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  stencil_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
  contract_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  selector_sig TEXT NOT NULL,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  first_chunk INTEGER NOT NULL,
  form_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  ssa_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  lower_ok INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  bank_id INTEGER UNIQUE,
  stencil_json TEXT,
  lower_errors_json TEXT
);
CREATE TABLE form_patterns (
  dedupe_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  ops_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  ops_json TEXT NOT NULL,
  count INTEGER NOT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY(dedupe_key, ops_key)
);
CREATE TABLE chunk_stats (
  chunk INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  seqs INTEGER NOT NULL,
  compiles INTEGER NOT NULL,
  ok INTEGER NOT NULL,
  ssa_ok INTEGER NOT NULL,
  rejected INTEGER NOT NULL,
  forms INTEGER NOT NULL,
  stencils INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE TABLE bank_stats (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  value INTEGER NOT NULL
);
BEGIN IMMEDIATE;
]]) == sqlite3.OK)

local insert_unique = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO unique_forms
  (dedupe_key, stencil_hash, contract_key, selector_sig, count, first_chunk, form_json, ssa_json)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
  ON CONFLICT(dedupe_key) DO UPDATE SET count = count + excluded.count]]))
local insert_pattern = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO form_patterns
  (dedupe_key, ops_key, ops_json, count) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
  ON CONFLICT(dedupe_key, ops_key) DO UPDATE SET count = count + excluded.count]]))
local insert_chunk = assert(db:prepare([[INSERT INTO chunk_stats
  (chunk, seqs, compiles, ok, ssa_ok, rejected, forms, stencils)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)]]))

local totals = {seqs = 0, compiles = 0, ok = 0, ssa_ok = 0, rejected = 0, forms = 0, stencils = 0, patterns = 0}

for ci = 1, chunks do
  local path = worker_path(ci)
  assert(Util.path_exists(path), "missing worker db: " .. path)
  local w = assert(sqlite3.open(path))
  local stats = {}
  for row in w:nrows("SELECT key, value FROM stats") do stats[row.key] = tonumber(row.value) or 0 end
  insert_chunk:bind_values(ci, stats.seqs or 0, stats.compiles or 0, stats.ok or 0, stats.ssa_ok or 0, stats.rejected or 0, stats.forms or 0, stats.stencils or 0)
  step_done_for(db, insert_chunk)
  totals.seqs = totals.seqs + (stats.seqs or 0)
  totals.compiles = totals.compiles + (stats.compiles or 0)
  totals.ok = totals.ok + (stats.ok or 0)
  totals.ssa_ok = totals.ssa_ok + (stats.ssa_ok or 0)
  totals.rejected = totals.rejected + (stats.rejected or 0)
  totals.forms = totals.forms + (stats.forms or 0)
  totals.stencils = totals.stencils + (stats.stencils or 0)

  local n = 0
  for row in w:nrows("SELECT dedupe_key, stencil_hash, contract_key, selector_sig, count, form_json, ssa_json FROM forms ORDER BY rowid") do
    insert_unique:bind_values(row.dedupe_key, row.stencil_hash, row.contract_key, row.selector_sig, tonumber(row.count) or 1, ci, row.form_json, row.ssa_json)
    step_done_for(db, insert_unique)
    n = n + 1
    if n % 50000 == 0 then io.stderr:write(string.format("[dedupe] chunk %d merged %d forms\n", ci, n)) end
  end

  local pn = 0
  for row in w:nrows("SELECT dedupe_key, ops_key, ops_json, count FROM patterns ORDER BY rowid") do
    insert_pattern:bind_values(row.dedupe_key, row.ops_key, row.ops_json, tonumber(row.count) or 1)
    step_done_for(db, insert_pattern)
    pn = pn + 1
  end
  totals.patterns = totals.patterns + pn
  w:close()
  io.stderr:write(string.format("[dedupe] chunk %d/%d forms=%d patterns=%d compiles=%d\n", ci, chunks, stats.forms or 0, pn, stats.compiles or 0))
end

insert_unique:finalize()
insert_pattern:finalize()
insert_chunk:finalize()
assert(db:exec("COMMIT;") == sqlite3.OK)

assert(db:exec([[
CREATE INDEX unique_forms_stencil_hash_idx ON unique_forms(stencil_hash);
CREATE INDEX unique_forms_count_idx ON unique_forms(count);
CREATE INDEX unique_forms_bank_id_idx ON unique_forms(bank_id);
CREATE INDEX form_patterns_ops_key_idx ON form_patterns(ops_key);
CREATE INDEX form_patterns_dedupe_key_idx ON form_patterns(dedupe_key);
BEGIN IMMEDIATE;
]]) == sqlite3.OK)

local update_lower = assert(db:prepare([[UPDATE unique_forms
  SET lower_ok = ?, bank_id = ?, stencil_json = ?, lower_errors_json = ? WHERE id = ?]]))
local unique_forms, lowered_stencils, lower_rejected = 0, 0, 0
for row in db:nrows("SELECT id, form_json, ssa_json FROM unique_forms ORDER BY id") do
  unique_forms = unique_forms + 1
  local id = tonumber(row.id)
  local form = assert(cjson.decode(row.form_json))
  local ssa = rehydrate_ssa_payload(assert(cjson.decode(row.ssa_json)))
  local lower = StencilLower.generate(ssa, { facts = form.facts or ssa.facts or {} })
  if lower.ok then
    local stencil = lower.stencil
    lowered_stencils = lowered_stencils + 1
    stencil.stencil_id = lowered_stencils
    stencil.abi = StencilDesc.lower_to_abi(stencil)
    update_lower:bind_values(1, lowered_stencils, cjson.encode(stencil), nil, id)
  else
    update_lower:bind_values(0, nil, nil, cjson.encode(lower.errors or {}), id)
    lower_rejected = lower_rejected + 1
  end
  step_done_for(db, update_lower)
  if unique_forms % 50000 == 0 then
    io.stderr:write(string.format("[dedupe] lowered %d unique forms, stencils=%d rejected=%d\n", unique_forms, lowered_stencils, lower_rejected))
  end
end
update_lower:finalize()
assert(db:exec("COMMIT;") == sqlite3.OK)

local pattern_aliases = 0
for row in db:nrows("SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM form_patterns") do pattern_aliases = tonumber(row.n) or 0 end

local stat_stmt = assert(db:prepare("INSERT INTO bank_stats(key, value) VALUES (?, ?)"))
local function stat(k, v)
  stat_stmt:bind_values(k, v or 0)
  step_done_for(db, stat_stmt)
end
stat("chunks", chunks)
stat("seqs", totals.seqs)
stat("compiles", totals.compiles)
stat("ok", totals.ok)
stat("ssa_ok", totals.ssa_ok)
stat("ssa_rejected", totals.rejected)
stat("worker_unique_forms", totals.forms)
stat("unique_forms", unique_forms)
stat("lowered_stencils", lowered_stencils)
stat("lower_rejected", lower_rejected)
stat("pattern_aliases", pattern_aliases)
stat_stmt:finalize()
db:close()

io.stderr:write(string.format("[dedupe] DONE chunks=%d compiles=%d worker_forms=%d unique_forms=%d lowered_stencils=%d pattern_aliases=%d db=%s\n",
  chunks, totals.compiles, totals.forms, unique_forms, lowered_stencils, pattern_aliases, out_path))

-- build_bank.lua -- Generate a C metadata bank from the global SQLite form DB.
--
-- This consumes build/stencil_bank/stencil_bank.sqlite from build_stencils.sh
-- and emits libsponbank.so exposing the sponbank.h descriptor/selector API.
-- Native stencil rows carry raw byte bodies plus explicit data/control relocs.

package.path = "../../../?.lua;../../../?/init.lua;src/?.lua;src/?/init.lua;" .. package.path

local cjson = require("cjson")
local sqlite3 = require("lsqlite3")
local Util = require("src.util")
local Constants = require("experiments.lua_interpreter_vm.src.constants")

local M = {}

local U32 = 4294967296

local function q(s) return Util.shell_quote(s) end

local function mkdir_p(path) assert(Util.mkdir_p(path), "mkdir failed: " .. tostring(path)) end

local function c_string(s)
  s = tostring(s or "")
  s = s:gsub("\\", "\\\\"):gsub('"', '\\"'):gsub("\n", "\\n"):gsub("\r", "\\r"):gsub("\t", "\\t")
  return '"' .. s .. '"'
end

local function as_c_u64_literal(x)
  if type(x) == "string" and x:match("^0x[%x]+ULL$") then return x end
  if type(x) == "string" and x:match("^0x[%x]+$") then return x .. "ULL" end
  return "0x0000000000000000ULL"
end

local function parse_sig_lohi(lit)
  local hex = tostring(lit or "0"):match("^0x([%x]+)ULL$") or tostring(lit or "0"):match("^0x([%x]+)$") or "0"
  hex = hex:lower()
  if #hex > 16 then hex = hex:sub(#hex - 15) end
  hex = string.rep("0", 16 - #hex) .. hex
  return tonumber(hex:sub(9, 16), 16) or 0, tonumber(hex:sub(1, 8), 16) or 0
end

local function popcount32(x)
  x = tonumber(x) or 0
  local n = 0
  while x > 0 do
    if x % 2 >= 1 then n = n + 1 end
    x = math.floor(x / 2)
  end
  return n
end

local function sig_pop(lit)
  local lo, hi = parse_sig_lohi(lit)
  return popcount32(lo) + popcount32(hi)
end

local OPCODE = {}
for name, value in pairs(Constants.Op or {}) do
  if type(name) == "string" and type(value) == "number" then OPCODE[name] = value end
end

local function op_name(op)
  if type(op) == "table" then return tostring(op.op or "") end
  return tostring(op or "")
end

local function packed_pattern(ops)
  local packed = 0
  for i, op in ipairs(ops or {}) do
    if i > 4 then break end
    local code = OPCODE[op_name(op)] or 0
    packed = packed + code * (256 ^ (i - 1))
  end
  return packed
end

local function selector_key_num(ops)
  local len = #(ops or {})
  return len * U32 + packed_pattern(ops)
end

local function selector_key_lit(ops)
  local len = #(ops or {})
  return string.format("0x%x%08xULL", len, packed_pattern(ops))
end

local function chunk_path(bank_src, name)
  if name:sub(1, 1) == "/" then return name end
  return bank_src .. "/" .. name
end

local function open_inc(out_dir, prefix, suffix)
  local path = string.format("%s/%s_%s.inc", out_dir, prefix, suffix)
  local f = assert(io.open(path, "wb"))
  return f, path
end

local function write_array_header(c, ctype, name, dummy)
  c:write("const ", ctype, " ", name, "[] = {\n")
  if dummy then c:write("  ", dummy, "\n") end
end

local function write_include(c, path)
  c:write('#include "', Util.basename(path), '"\n')
end

local function write_array_footer(c)
  c:write("};\n\n")
end

local function hex_to_bytes(hex)
  if type(hex) ~= "string" then return {} end
  local out = {}
  for pair in hex:gmatch("%x%x") do out[#out + 1] = tonumber(pair, 16) or 0 end
  return out
end

local function u(v) return tonumber(v) or 0 end
local function field(obj, name, default) local v = obj and obj[name]; if v == nil then return default end; return v end

local function emit_index(out_dir, shards, patterns, l0, total_stencils)
  local pattern_keys = {}
  for k, _ in pairs(patterns) do pattern_keys[#pattern_keys + 1] = k end
  table.sort(pattern_keys)

  local c_path = out_dir .. "/bank_index.c"
  local c = assert(io.open(c_path, "wb"))
  c:write("/* Generated by src/build_bank.lua. Native stencil bank index. */\n")
  c:write('#include "sponbank.h"\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n\n')

  for _, s in ipairs(shards) do
    local i = s.chunk
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponStencilDesc spon_chunk_%d_stencils[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const unsigned char spon_chunk_%d_data[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponEndpointDesc spon_chunk_%d_endpoints[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponLocationDesc spon_chunk_%d_locations[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponDataReloc spon_chunk_%d_data_relocs[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponControlReloc spon_chunk_%d_control_relocs[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponSlotMapEntry spon_chunk_%d_slotmaps[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponProjectionEntry spon_chunk_%d_projections[];\n", i))
    c:write(string.format("extern const SponDependencyDesc spon_chunk_%d_dependencies[];\n", i))
  end

  c:write([[

typedef struct {
  uint32_t first;
  uint32_t count;
  const SponStencilDesc *stencils;
  const unsigned char *data;
  const SponEndpointDesc *endpoints;
  const SponLocationDesc *locations;
  const SponDataReloc *data_relocs;
  const SponControlReloc *control_relocs;
  const SponSlotMapEntry *slotmaps;
  const SponProjectionEntry *projections;
  const SponDependencyDesc *dependencies;
} SponBankShard;

]])

  c:write("static const SponBankShard spon_shards[] = {\n")
  for _, s in ipairs(shards) do
    local i = s.chunk
    c:write(string.format("  {%d, %d, spon_chunk_%d_stencils, spon_chunk_%d_data, spon_chunk_%d_endpoints, spon_chunk_%d_locations, spon_chunk_%d_data_relocs, spon_chunk_%d_control_relocs, spon_chunk_%d_slotmaps, spon_chunk_%d_projections, spon_chunk_%d_dependencies},\n",
      s.first, s.count, i, i, i, i, i, i, i, i, i))
  end
  c:write("};\n")
  c:write(string.format("static const uint32_t spon_shard_count = %d;\n", #shards))
  c:write(string.format("static const uint32_t spon_stencil_count = %d;\n\n", total_stencils))

  c:write("static const SponStencilId spon_l0[256] = {\n")
  for i = 0, 255 do
    if i % 8 == 0 then c:write("  ") end
    c:write(tostring(l0[i] or 0))
    if i ~= 255 then c:write(", ") end
    if i % 8 == 7 then c:write("\n") end
  end
  c:write("};\n\n")

  c:write("typedef struct { uint64_t pattern_key; uint32_t start; uint32_t count; } PatternEntry;\n")
  c:write("static const SponStencilId spon_candidates[] = {\n")
  local candidate_count = 0
  for _, k in ipairs(pattern_keys) do
    local list = patterns[k]
    table.sort(list, function(a, b) return a > b end)
    for _, packed in ipairs(list) do
      local id = math.floor(packed % U32)
      if candidate_count % 12 == 0 then c:write("  ") end
      c:write(tostring(id), ", ")
      candidate_count = candidate_count + 1
      if candidate_count % 12 == 0 then c:write("\n") end
    end
  end
  if candidate_count % 12 ~= 0 then c:write("\n") end
  c:write("};\n")

  c:write("static const PatternEntry spon_patterns[] = {\n")
  local start = 0
  for _, k in ipairs(pattern_keys) do
    local list = patterns[k]
    local len = math.floor(k / U32)
    local packed = math.floor(k - len * U32)
    c:write(string.format("  {0x%x%08xULL, %d, %d},\n", len, packed, start, #list))
    start = start + #list
  end
  c:write("};\n")
  c:write(string.format("static const uint32_t spon_pattern_count = %d;\n\n", #pattern_keys))

  c:write([[
static const SponBankShard *find_shard(SponStencilId id) {
  uint32_t lo = 0, hi = spon_shard_count;
  while (lo < hi) {
    uint32_t mid = lo + ((hi - lo) >> 1);
    const SponBankShard *s = &spon_shards[mid];
    if (id < s->first) hi = mid;
    else if (id >= s->first + s->count) lo = mid + 1;
    else return s;
  }
  return NULL;

[497 more lines in file. Use offset=221 to continue.]

inc.slotmaps = select(1, open_inc(out_dir, prefix, "slotmaps"))
  inc.projections = select(1, open_inc(out_dir, prefix, "projections"))
  inc.dependencies = select(1, open_inc(out_dir, prefix, "dependencies"))

  local counts = {
    stencils = 0,
    bytes = 1,
    endpoints = 0,
    locations = 0,
    data_relocs = 0,
    control_relocs = 0,
    slotmaps = 0,
    projections = 0,
    dependencies = 0,
  }

  local sql = string.format("SELECT bank_id AS id, stencil_json FROM unique_forms WHERE lower_ok = 1 AND stencil_json IS NOT NULL AND bank_id >= %d AND bank_id <= %d ORDER BY bank_id", first_id, last_id)
  for row in db:nrows(sql) do
    local stencil_id = tonumber(row.id)
    local stencil = assert(cjson.decode(row.stencil_json))
    local abi = assert(stencil.abi, "stencil row missing abi")
    local af = assert(abi.stencil, "stencil row missing abi.stencil")
    local len = u(af.len ~= nil and af.len or stencil.len)
    local pattern_lit = as_c_u64_literal(af.pattern_key)
    local selector_sig = as_c_u64_literal(af.selector_sig)
    local required_sig = as_c_u64_literal(af.required_sig)
    local checked_sig = as_c_u64_literal(af.checked_sig)
    local produced_sig = as_c_u64_literal(af.produced_sig)
    local killed_sig = as_c_u64_literal(af.killed_sig)

    local code_bytes = hex_to_bytes(stencil.code_hex or abi.code_hex)
    local byte_start = (#code_bytes > 0) and counts.bytes or 0
    local byte_size = #code_bytes
    local endpoints = abi.endpoints or {}
    local locations = abi.locations or {}
    local data_relocs = abi.data_relocs or {}
    local control_relocs = abi.control_relocs or {}
    local slotmaps = abi.slotmaps or {}
    local projections = abi.projections or {}
    local dependencies = abi.dependencies or {}

    local endpoint_start = (#endpoints > 0) and (counts.endpoints + 1) or 0
    local location_start = (#locations > 0) and (counts.locations + 1) or 0
    local data_reloc_start = (#data_relocs > 0) and (counts.data_relocs + 1) or 0
    local control_reloc_start = (#control_relocs > 0) and (counts.control_relocs + 1) or 0
    local slotmap_start = (#slotmaps > 0) and (counts.slotmaps + 1) or 0
    local projection_start = (#projections > 0) and (counts.projections + 1) or 0
    local dependency_start = (#dependencies > 0) and (counts.dependencies + 1) or 0

    inc.stencils:write(string.format(
      "  {%d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, 0, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s}, /* %s */\n",
      stencil_id, byte_start, byte_size, endpoint_start, data_reloc_start, control_reloc_start, slotmap_start, projection_start,
      dependency_start, len, #endpoints, #data_relocs, #control_relocs, #slotmaps, #projections, #dependencies,
      u(af.flags or 1), u(af.physical_abi or 1), pattern_lit, selector_sig, required_sig, checked_sig, produced_sig,
      killed_sig, tostring(stencil.name or "stencil")))

    for i, b in ipairs(code_bytes) do
      if counts.bytes % 16 == 0 then inc.data_bytes:write("  ") end
      inc.data_bytes:write(string.format("0x%02x, ", b))
      counts.bytes = counts.bytes + 1
      if counts.bytes % 16 == 0 then inc.data_bytes:write("\n") end
    end
    if #code_bytes > 0 and counts.bytes % 16 ~= 0 then inc.data_bytes:write("\n") end

    for _, loc in ipairs(locations) do
      inc.locations:write(string.format("  {%d, %d, %d, 0, %d},\n", u(loc.kind), u(loc.value_type), u(loc.reg), u(loc.index)))
      counts.locations = counts.locations + 1
    end
    for _, ep in ipairs(endpoints) do
      local ep_loc_start = u(ep.location_start)
      if ep_loc_start > 0 then ep_loc_start = location_start + ep_loc_start - 1 end
      local ep_proj_start = u(ep.projection_start)
      if ep_proj_start > 0 then ep_proj_start = projection_start + ep_proj_start - 1 end
      inc.endpoints:write(string.format("  {%d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d},\n", u(ep.kind), u(ep.flags), ep_loc_start, u(ep.n_locations), 0, ep_proj_start, u(ep.n_projections)))
      counts.endpoints = counts.endpoints + 1
    end
    for _, r in ipairs(data_relocs) do
      inc.data_relocs:write(string.format("  {%d, %d, %d, %d, %d, %d},\n", u(r.code_offset), u(r.reloc_kind), u(r.role_kind), u(r.op_idx), u(r.role_arg), u(r.addend)))
      counts.data_relocs = counts.data_relocs + 1
    end
    for _, r in ipairs(control_relocs) do
      inc.control_relocs:write(string.format("  {%d, %d, %d, %d, %d},\n", u(r.code_offset), u(r.reloc_kind), u(r.edge_kind), u(r.endpoint_index), u(r.target_delta)))
      counts.control_relocs = counts.control_relocs + 1
    end
    for _, sm in ipairs(slotmaps) do
      inc.slotmaps:write(string.format("  {%d, %d, %d},\n", u(sm.op_idx), u(sm.logical_slot), u(sm.field_kind)))
      counts.slotmaps = counts.slotmaps + 1
    end
    for _, p in ipairs(projections) do
      inc.projections:write(string.format("  {%d, %d, %d, %d, %d},\n", u(p.kind), u(p.value_type), u(p.logical_slot), u(p.value_index), u(p.pc)))
      counts.projections = counts.projections + 1
    end
    for _, d in ipairs(dependencies) do
      inc.dependencies:write(string.format("  {%s},\n", c_string(d.name or d[1] or "")))
      counts.dependencies = counts.dependencies + 1
    end

    counts.stencils = counts.stencils + 1
    if counts.stencils % 10000 == 0 then io.stderr:write(string.format("[bank] shard %d stencils=%d\n", shard_index, counts.stencils)) end
  end
  for _, f in pairs(inc) do f:close() end

  local c_path = string.format("%s/%s.c", out_dir, prefix)
  local c = assert(io.open(c_path, "wb"))
  c:write("/* Generated by src/build_bank.lua. Native stencil bank shard. */\n")
  c:write('#include "sponbank.h"\n#include <stddef.h>\n#include <stdint.h>\n\n')
  write_array_header(c, "SponStencilDesc", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_stencils", shard_index), nil)
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "stencils")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "unsigned char", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_data", shard_index), "0,")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "data")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "SponEndpointDesc", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_endpoints", shard_index), "{0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0},")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "endpoints")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "SponLocationDesc", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_locations", shard_index), "{0, 0, 0, 0, 0},")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "locations")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "SponDataReloc", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_data_relocs", shard_index), "{0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0},")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "data_relocs")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "SponControlReloc", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_control_relocs", shard_index), "{0, 0, 0, 0, 0},")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "control_relocs")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "SponSlotMapEntry", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_slotmaps", shard_index), "{0, 0, 0},")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "slotmaps")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "SponProjectionEntry", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_projections", shard_index), "{0, 0, 0, 0, 0},")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "projections")); write_array_footer(c)
  write_array_header(c, "SponDependencyDesc", string.format("spon_chunk_%d_dependencies", shard_index), "{NULL},")
  write_include(c, string.format("%s_%s.inc", prefix, "dependencies")); write_array_footer(c)
  c:close()

  return {
    chunk = shard_index,
    first = first_id,
    count = counts.stencils,
    bytes = counts.bytes,
    endpoints = counts.endpoints,
    locations = counts.locations,
    data_relocs = counts.data_relocs,
    control_relocs = counts.control_relocs,
    slotmaps = counts.slotmaps,
    projections = counts.projections,
    dependencies = counts.dependencies,
    c_file = Util.basename(c_path),
  }
end

local function add_pattern_candidate(patterns, l0, ops, stencil_id, selector_sig)
  local pattern_num = selector_key_num(ops)
  local score = sig_pop(selector_sig)
  local list = patterns[pattern_num]
  if not list then list = {}; patterns[pattern_num] = list end
  list[#list + 1] = score * U32 + stencil_id

  if #(ops or {}) == 1 then
    local opcode = OPCODE[op_name(ops[1])] or nil
    if opcode and opcode >= 0 and opcode < 256 then
      if selector_sig == "0x0000000000000000ULL" or not l0[opcode] then l0[opcode] = stencil_id end
    end
  end
end

local function load_pattern_aliases(db, patterns, l0)
  local n = 0
  local sql = [[SELECT u.bank_id AS id, u.selector_sig AS selector_sig, p.ops_json AS ops_json
    FROM unique_forms u JOIN form_patterns p ON p.dedupe_key = u.dedupe_key
    WHERE u.lower_ok = 1 AND u.stencil_json IS NOT NULL AND u.bank_id IS NOT NULL
    ORDER BY p.ops_key, u.id]]
  for row in db:nrows(sql) do
    add_pattern_candidate(patterns, l0, assert(cjson.decode(row.ops_json)), tonumber(row.id), as_c_u64_literal(row.selector_sig))
    n = n + 1
    if n % 100000 == 0 then io.stderr:write(string.format("[bank] loaded %d pattern aliases\n", n)) end
  end
  return n
end

function M.build(bank_src, out_dir)
  bank_src = bank_src or "build/stencil_bank"
  out_dir = out_dir or "build/sponbank"
  mkdir_p(out_dir)

  local db_path = bank_src
  if not tostring(db_path):match("%.sqlite$") then db_path = bank_src .. "/stencil_bank.sqlite" end
  assert(Util.path_exists(db_path), "missing SQLite stencil bank: " .. db_path)
  local db = assert(sqlite3.open(db_path))

  local total_stencils, max_id = 0, 0
  for row in db:nrows("SELECT COUNT(*) AS n, COALESCE(MAX(bank_id), 0) AS max_id FROM unique_forms WHERE lower_ok = 1 AND stencil_json IS NOT NULL AND bank_id IS NOT NULL") do
    total_stencils = tonumber(row.n) or 0
    max_id = tonumber(row.max_id) or 0
  end
  assert(total_stencils > 0, "stencil_bank.sqlite has no lowered stencil rows")

  local rows_per_shard = tonumber(os.getenv("BANK_ROWS_PER_SHARD") or "50000") or 50000
  local patterns = {}
  local l0 = {}
  local shards = {}
  local t0 = os.time()
  local shard_index = 1
  local first_id = 1
  while first_id <= max_id do
    local last_id = math.min(max_id, first_id + rows_per_shard - 1)
    io.stderr:write(string.format("[bank] generating shard %d ids=%d..%d\n", shard_index, first_id, last_id))
    local shard = emit_sqlite_shard(db, out_dir, shard_index, first_id, last_id, patterns, l0)
    if shard.count > 0 then shards[#shards + 1] = shard end
    first_id = last_id + 1
    shard_index = shard_index + 1
  end
  table.sort(shards, function(a, b) return a.first < b.first end)

  io.stderr:write("[bank] loading pattern aliases\n")
  local pattern_aliases = load_pattern_aliases(db, patterns, l0)
  db:close()

  io.stderr:write("[bank] generating index/selector\n")
  local index = emit_index(out_dir, shards, patterns, l0, total_stencils)

  local summary = {
    kind = "sponjit.c_stencil_bank_build.v1",
    source = db_path,
    output = out_dir,
    stencils = total_stencils,
    max_id = max_id,
    rows_per_shard = rows_per_shard,
    chunks = #shards,
    patterns = index.patterns,
    candidates = index.candidates,
    pattern_aliases = pattern_aliases,
    seconds = os.time() - t0,
    shards = shards,
    index = index,
  }
  assert(Util.write_json(out_dir .. "/sponbank_build_manifest.json", summary))
  io.stderr:write(string.format("[bank] stencils=%d patterns=%d candidates=%d shards=%d done in %ds\n", summary.stencils, summary.patterns, summary.candidates, summary.chunks, summary.seconds))
  return summary
end

if arg and arg[0] and tostring(arg[0]):match("build_bank%.lua$") then
  M.build(arg[1] or "build/stencil_bank", arg[2] or "build/sponbank")
end

return M
